data.body:
4th SECURITY FINDING (664 LLM-Review -1, verify_opcert.py:163): the merge-gate checks the cert subject against material_fingerprint/merged_log_commit READ FROM THE SAME STORED RECORD the envelope came from (self-reported, attacker-influenceable on the auto-pushed tickets branch) — a circular check. This is the VERIFY-SIDE TWIN of ee0b's authoritative-input problem: the gate must recompute the authoritative material fingerprint (current_material_fingerprint from live ticket state) AND establish the authoritative commit-under-test, not trust the record's self-reported values. The merged_log_commit half is COUPLED to the produce-side (ee0b) design under escalation -> NOT a clean standalone patch. Folding into the escalation: 4214 also needs the authoritative-input fix; it is part of the same authoritative-state trust-model design the stronger model must resolve across produce (ee0b) + verify (4214).
STATUS: 660 (e4df) LLM+1 now (kind-confusion cleared) — closeable once Verified green. 649/650/660 clean (LLM+1), Verified flaky (rechecked). 664 blocked on this 4th finding (design-coupled).
